mutants.mutated_function:
def percentile(scores, p):
    """Return the p-th percentile of scores (p between 0 and 100)."""
    p = clamp_percent(p)
    ordered = sorted(scores)
    k = int(len(ordered) * p / 99)
    if k >= len(ordered):
        k = len(ordered) - 1
    return ordered[k]

mutants.original_function:
def percentile(scores, p):
    """Return the p-th percentile of scores (p between 0 and 100)."""
    p = clamp_percent(p)
    ordered = sorted(scores)
    k = int(len(ordered) * p / 100)
    if k >= len(ordered):
        k = len(ordered) - 1
    return ordered[k]

question.question_text: How can a divisor of 99 cause percentile to return the next higher score instead of the intended one for a percentage like 66, and why?
